requirements.title: Isolated multi-profile browsing
Ephemeral private sessions
Crash-safe tab and window session recovery
History bookmarks downloads and settings surfaces
Visible tab lifecycle and protection state
Turing-owned HTML tokenizer and tree builder
Turing-owned DOM and event model
Turing-owned CSS parser cascade and computed values
Turing-owned layout fragment system
Turing-owned paint display-list and hit-test pipeline
Accessibility tree generated from engine semantics
No existing browser engine in page-rendering path
Turing-owned ECMAScript frontend and interpreter
Exact tracing garbage collector with stress verification
Generated Web IDL bindings with lifecycle checks
Baseline JIT gated by tier equivalence and W^X
No-JIT hardened mode
WebAssembly validation and execution track
Renderer has no unrestricted socket access
Kernel-validated request context carries profile origin site and epoch
HTTP 1.1 TLS redirects cache cookies and Fetch subset
CORS CSP CORP COOP COEP and mixed-content enforcement
Profile and origin partitioned storage broker
Transactional versioned storage migrations with fault tests
Quota accounting eviction and clear-site-data correctness
OS-sandboxed untrusted renderer processes
Site isolation by browsing-context group and site instance
Typed bounded deny-by-default IPC
Signed reproducible rollback-capable updates
Private vulnerability response before developer preview
Versioned reproducible startup interaction memory and energy benchmarks
Thirty-tab mixed and all-live scenarios disclose lifecycle and isolation
Per-principal CPU memory GPU network disk and agent accounting
Bounded queues and cache budgets
Native browser UI accessibility on supported platforms
Web content semantics bridged to platform accessibility APIs
Versioned DevTools protocol derived from engine truth
Headless mode uses the same engine and security model
WebDriver BiDi compatibility track
Agents are separately identified capability principals
Default agent observations exclude secrets and ungranted cross-origin content
Every agent action validates origin profile grant and document epoch
Consequential agent actions require deterministic confirmation policy
Visible stop revoke and local audit for agent sessions
SBOM provenance symbols notices and release evidence share build identity
Supported versions and emergency patch capacity precede stable release
